title: Atmosphere
author: Taylor Jenkins Reid
description: TJR's books can lean romantic and a little impractical but this one surprised me by being so much more grounded than that, and better for it. She had to research and interview real experts to get the space details right and it shows — there's real substance here, I cried, I laughed, and I learned things. Maybe I'm underrating this one but there are just a few others I love more.

title: The Correspondent
author: Virginia Evans
description: This book is a perfect ten and I will die on that hill. The entire story is told through letters and emails and I was obsessed with every single one of them, from the relationships that made me laugh out loud (the customer service agent storyline is everything) to the ones that genuinely made me cry (her neighbor). Also I would really love for more men to read this one. It's that good.

title: Heart the Lover
author: Lily King
description: You will not be able to put this down and you will cry and you should be warned about that going in. Lily King writes love and life in a way that is so heartbreakingly real that you'll find yourself wanting to reach into the pages and shake the characters into making different decisions. Beautiful, devastating, and an easy read in the best possible way.

title: All Fours
author: Miranda July
description: Unhinged, a little cringey, and somehow completely relatable all at once. Miranda July wrote the kind of book that makes you laugh and then immediately feel seen in ways you weren't entirely prepared for. Real and funny and sad and a lot and I loved it.

title: Bunny
author: Mona Awad
description: People kept telling me Mean Girls meets Practical Magic and look, those are big compliments to throw at a book. I spent most of this one confused, never connected with a single character, and had to call in backup from friends just to figure out what actually happened. Not for me but it has a devoted following so maybe it's just that!

title: The Tell
author: Amy Griffin
description: A genuinely eye opening and important read especially if you're someone who feels deeply. It's not a light one and I wouldn't hand it to everyone but if you're ready to sit with some heavy and meaningful stuff this one really delivers.

title: Flesh
author: David Szalay
description: The main character starts as a Hungarian teenager having a secret affair with a married neighbor and somehow ends up in London's upper class by way of detention, war, and migration and you are rooting for him the entire time. It's such a nice change of pace to be in Budapest and London rather than the usual New York. The book tells a great depiction of life with barely any banter or dialogue and that's what is so impressive. You want more for him, but then again that's life and I think that's exactly the point. It leaves you sitting with it long after you've finished.

title: Strangers: A Memoir of Marriage
author: Belle Burden
description: Ooof! I hope this does NOT happen to me! It's a painfully real story about a man who leaves his wife and kids out of the blue and it will have you side-eyeing everyone you love and I say that with full affection. It's the kind of book where you see yourself in it even when you don't want to and Belle Burden writes the unraveling of a life she thought she knew with so much honesty that it's genuinely hard to shake. A lot of women are going to feel this one in their chest.

title: Signs: The Secret Language of the Universe
author: Laura Lynne Jackson
description: I picked this up at exactly the right moment in my life and I'm so glad I did. It's a beautiful reminder that we're all connected, that this life is so much bigger than it seems, and that the people we've lost are still out there in some way shaping our days. It leaves you with a kind of quiet optimism that I really needed.

title: Journey of Souls: Case Studies of Life Between Lives
author: Michael Newton
description: I really loved learning from this one and I do believe the science behind what Newton is exploring. My only thing is I wanted more magic and mystery from what comes after this life because parts of it felt almost too human. Like I'm hoping the other side has a little more going on than what's described here! Still a fascinating read.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
author: Mark Manson
description: Honestly everyone should read this at some point. It's a good reset for your values and a reminder that being selective about what you actually care about makes you a kinder and more intentional person. Less keeping up with the Joneses, more living like you mean it.

title: Night People: How to Be a DJ in '90s New York City
author: Mark Ronson
description: Do yourself a favor and get the audiobook because hearing Mark tell this story in his own voice is genuinely the move. It's full of personality, name drops, nostalgic New York spots, and the kind of energy that makes you wish you could go back in time. A really fun way to get to know the man behind so much music you already love.

title: Workhorse
author: Caroline Palmer
description: I loved every single page of this book and laughed out loud more than once. Full disclosure I know Caroline Palmer and she is genuinely one of the most creative and funniest people I know, and what makes this story so special is that she is completely herself on every page. The situations Clo gets herself into will give you full body anxiety but you are always always rooting for her to win. Fashion, friendship, personal growth, the real New York... it made me miss it all and I did not want it to end. Every woman in New York needs to read this bad baby.
